Re: Sal1y Anne Sheridan
l,layor of the city of Irvine

Irvine, cA 92713-9575
Dear lr{s . Stecher:
This letter requests formal written advice pursuant to
Governnent code section 83114 (b) and 2 california code of
Regulat.ions section 18329(a). us. Sheridan has authorized me to
nake this request on her behalf. The letter contains all facts
material to the consideration of the questions presented belolr.
we spoke on the telephone on october 4, L990, regarding the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(nActr) as they relate to factual scenario described below.
Though we spoke only in qeneral terms, you advised me at that
tine that the employer of an enployee who is a source of income
to a public official 
is not also a source of the official rs
incorne. This letter also requests rrritten confirrnaiion of that
oral advice.
Facts
llayor Sheridan is a real estate agent in Nehrport Beach. she
often represents individuats in real estate transactions rrho are
employees of companies with matters pending before the Irvine
City Council. The comrnissions she receives on these transactions
normally exceed $250.
Irlayor Sheridanrs husband is also a real estate agent in
Newport Beach. He also represents individuals tho are employees
of cornpanies with matters pending before the Irvine City bouncil .
Ilis comrnissions norrnally exceed $500.


JiIl stecher, Esq.
october 22, 1990
Paqe 2
The eDployees/clients do not hold olrnership interests in
their conpanier.
ouestionE
Uay llayor Sheridan participate in decisions of the City
Council irhich have a reasonably foreseeable, naterial financial
effect on companies rdhose enployees she has represented in real
e8tate transactions?
iiajr uayor siieridan par:icipate in decisions of the city
Council rrhich have a reasonably foreaeeable, naterial financial
effect on conpanies uhose ehployees her husband has represented
in real estate transactions?
Analvsis
2 California Code of Regulations section 18704.3 addresses
dj-squal i fication relative to incone earned as a real estate
agent. If the comDission the agent earns is over 9250, the
regulation states that the agent Dust disqualify hin or herself
fron participating in any decision which wouLd affect: (1) the
brokerage business rdhere the agent worksi (2) the perEon the
agen! represented in the transaction; and (3) any person to which
the agent paid a referral fee.
Applyinq this regulation to the facts at issue, t{ayor
Sheridan nust disqualify herself frou decisions affectl.ng her
clients, the brokerage business vhere she works, and anyone to
which she has paid a referral fee. The sane conclusion appliea
to clients of her husband. The Act does lqE require her,
horever, to disquaLj.fy herself fron participating in deciEions
affecting the enplovers of her, or her husbandrs, clients. This
conclusion appiies as Long as neither iiayor Sheridan nor her
husband hold any other financial interests in the coopanies
coning before the City Council.
Conclusi.on
As rnentioned above, llayor Sheridan and her husband have
several clients, plus several prospective clients, vho are
eTploygd by conpanies lrith natters before the City Council. She
viII thus face this situation regularly in the neir iuture and
need confirmation of her obligations u;der the political Reforn
Act.


Ji1I stecher, Esq.
october 22, l99o
Page 3
We appreciate your time and coneideration. P1ea6e caII me
if you need any further information.
sincerely,

Re: Letter No, 90-6?2
Dear Irts . Stecher:
we recently requested advice fron the r.PPc on the duties of
sally Anne Sheridan, the }Iayor of Irvine, under the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reforn Act. The l-etter was
written by me, on behalf of l.layor sheridan; we therefore assume
that the FPPC ari1l address its response to me.
As I rnentioned to you on the telephone last week, we would
Iike to change the requestor of the advice. our firm provides
Iegal advice on political 
law issues to the Irvine Clty Attorney,
rather than representing Mayor Sheridan directly. 
The city
Attorney has decided that all FPPC Advice Letters relating to
Irvine city officials 
should be addressed to hin,
You indicated that the FPPC could change the name of the
requestor as long as you received authorizatj-on fron the rrnewtl
and tro1dtr requestors. we would appreciate the FPPC addressing
its advice letter to John Fel1ows, Irvine City Attorney. Hj,s
authorization is being sent to you separately.
Thank you very nuch for your cooperation.
JRs/df
